params.groupId: The group the oracle is authorized for

params.initialGroupId: Group to authorize the initial oracle for
params.initialOracle: Initial oracle address to authorize (optional, can be address(0))
params.initialOwner: The initial owner of the contract

params.maxLevels: Maximum number of levels to traverse
params.user: The user to get ancestors for

returns._0: Array of ancestors, starting with immediate referrer
Array of authorized oracle addresses for the group
Array of addresses that were referred by this referrer

params.maxLevels: Maximum number of non-skiplisted ancestors to return
params.user: The user to get ancestors for

returns._0: Array of non-skiplisted ancestors, starting with the nearest eligible referrer

params.maxLevels: Maximum number of paid recipients to return
params.user: First candidate recipient (omitted if skiplisted; walk continues upward)

returns.chain: Non-skiplisted addresses from `user` upward, capped at `maxLevels`

returns._0: The address of the referrer, or address(0) if not registered

params.groupId: The group to check authorization for

returns._0: True if authorized for the group
True if the user has a referrer in the group

register(address,address,bytes32).details: Groups are implicitly created when the first user registers. A user is in a group's referral tree if they have been referred or have referred others.

params.groupId: The group ID (group is auto-created on first registration)
params.referrer: The referrer address (must be in the group's referral tree, or REFERRAL_ROOT for root registration)

setSkiplisted(address,bytes32,bool).details: Only callable by an oracle authorized for `groupId`

params.skiplisted: True to skiplist, false to remove

params.groupId: The group to remove authorization from

REFERRAL_ROOT().notice: Special address representing the root of all referral trees

authorizeOracle(address,bytes32).notice: Authorize an oracle to register referrals in a group

batchRegister(address[],address,bytes32).notice: Batch register multiple users with the same referrer in a group

getAncestors(address,bytes32,uint256).notice: Get the ancestor chain for a user in a group

getAuthorizedOracles(bytes32).notice: Get all authorized oracles for a group

getChildren(address,bytes32).notice: Get the children of a referrer in a group

getPayoutAncestors(address,bytes32,uint2.notice: Get ancestors with skiplisted addresses removed (does not count them toward maxLevels)

getPayoutChain(address,bytes32,uint256).notice: Build the payout chain starting from `user`, omitting skiplisted addresses

getReferrer(address,bytes32).notice: Get the referrer of a user in a group

getSkiplisted(bytes32).notice: Get all skiplisted addresses for a group

isAuthorizedOracle(address,bytes32).notice: Check if an address is an authorized oracle for a group

isRegistered(address,bytes32).notice: Check if a user is registered in a group

isSkiplisted(address,bytes32).notice: Check if an address is on the skip list for a group

register(address,address,bytes32).notice: Register a user with a referrer in a group

setSkiplisted(address,bytes32,bool).notice: Add or remove an address from a group's skip list

unauthorizeOracle(address,bytes32).notice: Unauthorize an oracle for a group
